name: Independent Code Review
description: Changes are reviewed by an independent code reviewer prior to code being merged and released to production. Independent code review restricts the ability for anyone individual from taking a change production without involving an independent party (4-eyes principle).

attributes.text: Code Reviews are performed prior to committing a change to the main branch
testable_criteria: A code review record (e.g. pull request) exists for the change.
The code review was completed and approved before the change was merged into the main branch.
The merge commit timestamp to main is after the code review approval timestamp.
No direct commits to main were made bypassing the code review process.

attributes.text: Code Review approvals are performed by independent code reviewers
testable_criteria: At least one approval on the code review is recorded from a reviewer other than the change author.
The approving reviewer is not the same identity (username/email) as the author or committer of the change.
The approving reviewer has authority/permission to approve code reviews in the repository.
The approval was not self-approved or granted by an automated bot acting on behalf of the author.

attributes.text: Testing is performed in accordance with the testing policy
testable_criteria: Unit test code coverage for new code is ≥ 80%.
Integration test coverage for critical integration points is ≥ 60%.
IF the change touches security-sensitive, payment, or authentication flows THEN line coverage for those paths = 100%.
IF the change modifies legacy code THEN existing coverage levels are maintained or improved.
Branch coverage is ≥ 70%.
Function coverage is ≥ 80%.
A coverage report was generated and reviewed as part of the code review.
All unit tests passed with zero failures prior to release.
All integration tests passed prior to release.
End-to-end tests for critical user journeys passed prior to release.
IF applicable, performance benchmarks were met prior to release.
Security scan results were reviewed and approved prior to release.
Tests were executed in the CI/CD pipeline before merge.
Test results are visible in the pull request status checks.
No known critical or high-severity bugs are open at time of release.
Manual smoke tests were completed for critical features prior to release.

IF the change qualifies for a documented exception (legacy integration, third-party dependency, PoC/experimental, documentation-only, build/deployment scripts, dependency version bump, or behavior-preserving refactor) THEN the exception is explicitly documented and applicable.
